title: Government responses to social movements
description: Explain how the government has responded to social movements

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best explains why the Civil Rights Act of 1964 was a significant political event? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It prohibited discrimination in public places, employment, and voting
It extended suffrage to African American men and women
It removed structural barriers to minority voting
It prevented schools and universities from discriminating against female students

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1963, Martin Luther King, Jr. organized the March on Washington to protest racial discrimination and segregation in public places. 

**Which of the following statements describes how Congress responded to this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress passed the Civil Rights Act of 1964
Congress passed the Voting Rights Act of 1965
Congress passed the Twenty-fourth Amendment
Congress passed the Equal Rights Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following policies is an example of specific legislation that extended civil rights to women?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Title IX of the Education Amendments Act of 1972
The Twenty-fourth Amendment
The Twenty-sixth Amendment
The Voting Rights Act of 1965

assessment_items.question_content:
In the 1960s, literacy tests were used in an attempt to limit African American participation in the voting process.

**Which of the following statements describes how Congress responded to this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress passed the Voting Rights Act of 1965
Congress passed the Civil Rights Act of 1964
Congress issued fines to any state who used literacy tests
Congress created the Federal Elections Commission to regulate voting centers

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best explains why the Voting Rights Act of 1965 was a significant political event?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It removed structural barriers to minority voting
It extended suffrage to African American men and women
It lowered the voting age from twenty-one to eighteen
It strengthened the role of primaries in presidential elections

assessment_items.question_content:
> “An unjust law is a code inflicted upon a minority which that minority had no part in enacting or creating because it did not have the unhampered right to vote. Who can say that the legislature of Alabama which set up the segregation laws was democratically elected? Throughout the state of Alabama all types of conniving methods are used to prevent Negroes from becoming registered voters, and there are some counties without a single Negro registered to vote, despite the fact that the Negroes constitute a majority of the population. Can any law set up in such a state be considered democratically structured?”

> Dr. Martin Luther King, “Letter from a Birmingham Jail,” 1963

**Which of the following governmental policies would the author most likely support as a solution to the problems described in this passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Voting Rights Act of 1965
The Civil Rights Act of 1964
Title IX of the Education Amendments Act of 1972
The Twenty-sixth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1954, the Supreme Court ruled that race-based segregation in a public school system was unconstitutional. 

**Which of the following constitutional provisions did the Supreme Court use to support their decision?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The equal protection clause of the Fourteenth Amendment
The due process clause of the Fourteenth Amendment
The free exercise clause clause of the First Amendment
The due process clause of the Fifth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1952, four African American students in Topeka, Kansas were denied access to certain public schools because of laws segregating public education by race. 

**Which of the following statements describes how the Supreme Court responded to this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supreme Court ruled that the “separate but equal” doctrine violated the equal protection clause of the Fourteenth Amendment
The Supreme Court ruled that “separate but equal” facilities did not violate the equal protection clause of the Fourteenth Amendment
The Supreme Court ruled that this was not a justiciable issue and refused to hear the case
The Supreme Court ruled that federal laws had supremacy over state laws and struck down Jim Crow laws

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of the two court cases?** 

 | *Brown v. Board of Education* (1954) | *Roe v. Wade* (1973) 
- | - | -
**A** | Incorporated the right to vote to the states | Incorporated the right to privacy to the states
**B** | Decided that segregation in schools violates the equal protection clause | Decided that the right to privacy extends to a woman’s right to have an abortion
**C** | Authorized states to create separate facilities based on race as long as they were equal | Limited the power of state governments to regulate abortion
**D** | Declared that the state had violated the Fourteenth Amendment | Declared that the state had violated the Sixth Amendment

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
In the 1960s and 1970s, women began protesting gender discrimination in colleges and universities, such as unequal scholarship allocation to men’s sports and women’s sports.

**Which of the following statements describes how Congress responded to this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress passed Title IX of the Education Amendments Act of 1972
Congress passed the Civil Rights Act of 1964
Congress passed the Equal Rights Amendment
Congress passed the Nineteenth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/128323999e3926f57c07e92cab9618fa.png)

**Which of the following accurately describes the information presented in the pie charts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Female athletes represented a larger proportion of total high school athletes in 2011-2012 than in 1971-1972
From 1971-72 to 2011-12, female athletes became more likely to participate in high school sports than male athletes
The total number of high school athletes decreased from 1971-72 to 2011-12
All high school sports became co-ed, allowing for female athletes to participate on male sports teams

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/128323999e3926f57c07e92cab9618fa.png)

**Based on the information in the pie charts, which of the following best summarizes the effect of Title IX?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It created more opportunities for female athletes to participate in sports
It decreased the number of male athletes involved in high school sports
It eliminated several male-dominated sports, like football, from American high schools
It increased the number of female scholarships in public and private universities
